title: Walking Pepe the Dog
summary: Two children in a Madrid park walk a small dog called Pepe and chat about his tricks, his food, and a stop at the fountain.

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: Hi Leo! Is this your new dog?

body.speaker: Leo
body.text: Yes! His name is Pepe. He is one year old.

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: He is so small and cute! Can I say hello?

body.speaker: Leo
body.text: Sure. Hold your hand low. He likes to smell first.

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: Good boy, Pepe! His tail is moving very fast.

body.speaker: Leo
body.text: That means he is happy. He likes new people.

body.speaker: Leo
body.text: He can sit and give his paw. Watch! Pepe, sit. Good boy!

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: Wow, he is clever! Where do you walk him?

body.speaker: Leo
body.text: We come to this park every afternoon. He loves the grass.

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: I want a dog too, but my flat is very small.

body.speaker: Leo
body.text: A small dog is okay for a flat. Pepe sleeps a lot at home.

body.speaker: Leo
body.text: Dry food in the morning and chicken in the evening. He loves chicken.

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: Look, he is pulling on the lead. Where does he want to go?

body.speaker: Leo
body.text: To the fountain. He always wants a drink there. Come with us!

annotations.exampleEn: Hold your hand low so the cat can smell it.
Dogs use their nose to smell everything.
The puppy has a short brown tail.
He can sit and give his paw.
My sister is very clever at school.
The children sat on the grass.
We live in a small flat near the park.
My cat eats dry food in the morning.
The dog is pulling on the lead. He wants to go faster.
We met at the fountain in the park.

grammarNotes.title: Using 'can' to talk about ability
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Can + động từ' nói về việc ai đó có khả năng làm gì: 'He can sit and give his paw.' / 'Can I say hello?' Câu phủ định là 'cannot' hoặc 'can't'. Đây là cách đơn giản nhất ở trình độ A1 để nói về kỹ năng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Can + verb' talks about ability: 'He can sit and give his paw.' / 'Can I say hello?' The negative is 'cannot' or 'can't'. This is the simplest A1 way to talk about skills.

grammarNotes.title: Present simple for routines with frequency words
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để nói về việc làm thường xuyên, ta dùng hiện tại đơn cùng các từ chỉ tần suất: 'We come to this park every afternoon.' / 'He always wants a drink there.' / 'Pepe sleeps a lot at home.' Các từ như 'every afternoon', 'always', 'a lot' cho biết tần suất.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Present simple with frequency words describes habits: 'We come to this park every afternoon.' / 'He always wants a drink there.' / 'Pepe sleeps a lot at home.' Words like 'every afternoon', 'always', and 'a lot' tell how often.

translationVi: Mia: Chào Leo! Đây là chú chó mới của bạn à? Leo: Đúng rồi! Tên nó là Pepe. Nó được một tuổi. Mia: Nó nhỏ và dễ thương quá! Mình chào nó được không? Leo: Được chứ. Bạn đưa tay xuống thấp. Nó thích ngửi trước. Mia: Ngoan nào, Pepe! Đuôi nó vẫy nhanh quá. Leo: Thế là nó đang vui đó. Nó thích người mới. Mia: Nó có biết trò nào không? Leo: Nó biết ngồi và đưa chân. Nhìn nè! Pepe, ngồi. Giỏi lắm! Mia: Ồ, nó thông minh quá! Bạn dắt nó đi dạo ở đâu? Leo: Chiều nào tụi mình cũng đến công viên này. Nó thích bãi cỏ. Mia: Mình cũng muốn có một con chó nhưng căn hộ mình nhỏ lắm. Leo: Chó nhỏ thì ở căn hộ vẫn được. Ở nhà Pepe ngủ nhiều lắm. Mia: Nó ăn gì? Leo: Buổi sáng ăn thức ăn khô, buổi tối ăn thịt gà. Nó mê gà lắm. Mia: Nhìn kìa, nó đang kéo dây dắt. Nó muốn đi đâu vậy? Leo: Tới đài phun nước. Nó luôn muốn uống nước ở đó. Đi với tụi mình đi!

questions.hint: Leo says it in his first answer.

questions.prompt: What does Pepe do first when he meets a new person?
questions.hint: Leo tells Mia what to do.

questions.prompt: How do we know Pepe is happy?
questions.explanation: 'His tail is moving very fast. That means he is happy.'

questions.prompt: What two tricks can Pepe do?
questions.explanation: 'He can sit and give his paw.'

questions.prompt: When does Leo walk the dog?
questions.explanation: 'We come to this park every afternoon.'

questions.prompt: Why does Mia not have a dog?
questions.explanation: 'I want a dog too, but my flat is very small.'

questions.prompt: What does Pepe eat in the evening?
questions.explanation: 'Dry food in the morning and chicken in the evening.'

questions.prompt: Where does Pepe want to go at the end?
Why does Pepe want to go there?
What does the word 'clever' mean in this dialogue?
questions.explanation: Mia says Pepe is clever because he knows tricks.
questions.hint: Mia uses it after seeing the tricks.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of this conversation?
options: Mia wants to buy a dog today
Mia meets Leo's dog and learns about him
They are looking for a lost dog
questions.explanation: Mia asks questions about Pepe and Leo answers them.
questions.hint: Think about why they are talking.

criticalThinkingQuestion: What is the most important thing to think about before getting a dog at home? Why?
